caption: The video appears to be an educational tutorial on cryogenic engineering, specifically focusing on the Linde-Hampson cycle. The slide displayed is titled "Tutorial - 1" and presents a problem related to determining the liquid yield for a Linde-Hampson cycle using air as the working fluid. The system operates between pressures of 1.013 bar (1 atm) and 202.6 bar (200 atm) at a temperature of 300 K. The effectiveness of the heat exchanger (HX) is varied at 100%, 95%, 90%, and 85%, with a prompt to comment on the results. Additionally, the slide includes a T-s diagram illustrating the Linde-Hampson system assuming a 100% effective heat exchanger. The diagram shows various states and processes, such as constant temperature and pressure lines, which help in visualizing the thermodynamic cycle. The tutorial is presented by Prof. M D Atrey from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Bombay, as indicated at the bottom of the slide.
